tests.title: Science Discovery Project
tests.subtitle: Organisers explain how this science project developed over time.
tests.text: Science Discovery Project began after several teenagers became interested in learning more about science. At first, meetings took place in a small community centre on Saturday afternoons. [11]The organisers originally believed only a small group of students would participate[/11]. However, after students shared photos and videos online, many more people wanted to join the activities. During the first few months, organisers had difficulty planning events because equipment and money were limited. Even so, members enjoyed learning practical skills together and helping each other improve. Older students soon started supporting beginners during activities and discussions. [12]Organisers noticed that beginners learned faster when experienced students explained tasks simply[/12]. Teachers and parents later commented that participants seemed more confident and independent outside school. Some former members even chose university courses connected with science. [13]The organisers were surprised that the programme affected students’ future study choices[/13]. Today, meetings usually begin with a short presentation before members work together on practical tasks. Participants are encouraged to ask questions and share their own ideas during sessions. Recently, organisers created online guides and videos so students can continue practising at home. [14]The organisers believe regular practice is more important than becoming successful immediately[/14]. Although the programme has grown larger, organisers still want new members to feel relaxed and welcome. Students are reminded that mistakes are a normal part of learning something new. [15]The main aim is to help young people feel confident about trying unfamiliar experiences[/15].

questions.question: What did organisers expect when the project first started?

options.A: The project would become famous internationally.
options.D: Only a few students would join.

questions.explanation: Organisers originally expected a small group.

questions.question: What helped beginners improve more quickly?
questions.explanation: Older students gave simple explanations and support.

questions.question: Why were organisers surprised later?
questions.explanation: The programme unexpectedly influenced study choices.

questions.question: What do organisers believe is important?
What is the main purpose of the programme?

options.C: Helping young people feel confident trying new things.

questions.explanation: The programme encourages confidence and participation.
